learning_objective: Students trace energy flow through a cogeneration system and compare total efficiency to conventional separate generation.
specification:
Type: diagram **sim-id:** cogeneration-flow
**Library:** vis-network
**Status:** Specified **Bloom Level:** Understand **Bloom Verb:** Explain **Learning Objective:** Students trace energy flow through a cogeneration system and compare total efficiency to conventional separate generation. **Instructional Rationale:** Sankey-style flow diagrams make energy losses visible, reinforcing the concept that efficiency means capturing energy that would otherwise be wasted. Flow diagram comparing two systems side by side. LEFT: Conventional -- fuel input (100 units) splits to Power Plant (33 units electricity, 67 units waste heat) and Boiler (80 units useful heat, 20 units waste). Total useful: 113 units from 200 units fuel input (56% efficient). RIGHT: Cogeneration -- fuel input (100 units) goes to CHP system producing 35 units electricity AND 50 units useful heat. Total useful: 85 units from 100 units fuel input (85% efficient). Waste heat shown in red, useful energy in green, fuel input in amber. Animated flow particles show energy movement.
